design_philosophy: Function-first, stark contrasts, and absolute clarity. The design relies on grid structure, pure white spaces, solid black outlines, and a single electric blue accent for primary actions. No rounded corners, no gradients.

hierarchy.h1: text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-black tracking-tighter text-[#0A0A0A]
hierarchy.label: text-xs font-bold tracking-[0.2em] uppercase text-[#525252]

layout_and_spacing.grid_system: Use bento grid for layout. Example: A main large column for the dropzone and a side column for settings/info, or a wide center container.
layout_and_spacing.borders: 1px solid #0A0A0A for primary structural elements, 1px solid #E5E5E5 for secondary dividers.

dropzone.base_classes: border-2 border-dashed border-[#0A0A0A] bg-[#FFFFFF] p-12 hover:bg-[#F9F9F9] transition-colors duration-200 cursor-pointer min-h-[300px] flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-4 text-center relative

buttons.primary: bg-[#0000FF] text-white px-6 py-3 font-semibold tracking-wide hover:bg-[#0000CC] transition-colors flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-none
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent text-[#0A0A0A] border border-[#0A0A0A] px-6 py-3 font-semibold hover:bg-[#0A0A0A] hover:text-white transition-colors flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-none
buttons.ghost: bg-transparent text-[#525252] hover:text-[#0A0A0A] hover:bg-black/5 px-4 py-2 transition-colors rounded-none

inputs.text: w-full border border-[#0A0A0A] bg-white px-4 py-3 text-base placeholder:text-[#A3A3A3] focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#0000FF] focus:border-transparent transition-all rounded-none
inputs.file_item: border-b border-[#E5E5E5] last:border-0 p-4 flex items-center justify-between bg-white hover:bg-[#F9F9F9] transition-colors

tabs.list: flex border-b border-[#0A0A0A] w-full bg-transparent rounded-none p-0
tabs.trigger: px-6 py-3 text-sm font-bold uppercase tracking-wider text-[#525252] hover:text-[#0A0A0A] border-b-2 border-transparent data-[state=active]:border-[#0000FF] data-[state=active]:text-[#0A0A0A] transition-all rounded-none

animations.interactions: Fast and snappy. 150ms-200ms ease-out for hovers.
animations.list_transitions: Staggered fade-in for file lists using Framer Motion.
animations.loading: Use an indeterminate progress bar with the electric blue accent for unlocking states.

icons.usage: Use stroke-width 1.5. Ensure icons in buttons are sized at 16px to 20px (w-4 h-4 to w-5 h-5).

hero_background.description: Abstract Swiss geometric design. Use this as a fixed background overlay (e.g., fixed inset-0 z-[-1] opacity-[0.03] object-cover w-full h-full mix-blend-multiply) to give the application a premium, editorial feel without distracting from the UI.

testing.requirements: All interactive elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case based on role (e.g., data-testid='upload-dropzone', data-testid='unlock-batch-button').

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup a single page application with two main tabs/modes: 'Single File' and 'Batch Unlock'.
2. In Single Mode: Provide a prominent drag-and-drop zone. Once a file is dropped, display a file card with a password input and an 'Unlock' button. On success, show a 'Download' button.
3. In Batch Mode: Allow selecting multiple files. Show a list of all selected files. Provide a global password input field above the list and an 'Unlock All' button.
4. For Batch Mode results: Once files are unlocked, provide a 'Download ZIP' button for the entire batch, alongside individual download icons for each file.

5. Use Shadcn UI for standard elements (Tabs, Inputs, Buttons) but heavily customize them to remove rounded corners (`rounded-none`) and apply the high-contrast border styles specified in the components section.

6. Do not implement complex user auth or backend history. Assume processing happens directly in the browser (e.g., using `pdf-lib` + `jszip` for zipping) or via a simplified simulated API flow if no specific backend exists.
7. Strictly adhere to the typography: Cabinet Grotesk for headings, IBM Plex Sans for body text. Insert @import rules in the global CSS.
8. Maintain generous padding and use pure structural lines (Swiss Brutalist / Grid approach).
9. Include the abstract Swiss background image as a subtle, ultra-low-opacity fixed background to elevate the visual aesthetic.
